<<Optimal Network Analysis>>

The minimum spanning tree constructed via Kruskal's algorithm includes the edges D-E (1), C-D (2), B-E (3), A-D (4), and E-F (4), yielding a total minimum cost of 14 miles to connect all distribution centers. Following this structure, the shortest path from distribution center A to distribution center F determined by Dijkstra's algorithm traverses the sequence A, D, E, and F with a total transportation cost of 9 miles.
